## Drift detected ã 2026-05-27 (slice 340 ã in-review + slice 341 filed)

Slice 340 investigation + fix landed: chromedp `wsURLReadTimeout` flake diagnosed (deterministic 20.0s clustering matches the chromedp hardcoded watchdog at `allocate.go:249`; cause is slice 117 Harden-Runner audit-mode stretching Chrome's component-updater startup network calls past the 20s threshold). Fix: `WSURLReadTimeout(60s)` + `DefaultTimeout` 30sã90s in `internal/policy/pdf/render.go`. `t.Skip` removed. Decisions log at `docs/audit-log/340-chromedp-flake-decisions.md`. Spillover slice 341 filed for fan-out to the other four PDF renderers (board/pdf, board/pack_pdf, questionnaire/pdf, audit/walkthrough/export) which share the same exposure.

| Row | Transition         | Evidence                                                                                                                         |
| --- | ------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 340 | `ready` ã `merged` | merged at `dd6f09d6` via #758 ôñ root cause: chromedp 20s wsURLReadTimeout watchdog ôñ fix: WSURLReadTimeout(60s) + 5û stress test |
| 341 | `ready` ã `merged` | merged at `36434fe5` via #761 ôñ 4-package WSURLReadTimeout(60s) fan-out ôñ D1 per-package not per-file (board has 2 renderers)    |

## Drift detected ã 2026-05-27 (slice 338 filed via /idea-to-slice)

Final of 12 voltagent-qa-sec audit slices filed in the 2026-05-27 audit-planning session. This is the only slice that exercises the platform externally ã runs `penetration-tester` agent against `https://atlas-edge.home.gmoney.sh` ONLY (the maintainer's authorized sandbox). Seven in-scope probe surfaces: OIDC auth, JWT handling (ES256 per ADR-0003), evidence push API, tenant isolation (RLS invariant #6), admin surfaces (slices 062/142/143), bootstrap (slice 210), atlas-edge-specific (slices 207-211). Strict P0 anti-criteria: no other hosts, no DoS, no social/physical, no persistence, no lateral movement, no exploit payloads in public log, authorization grant recorded before run. Security ôñ JUDGMENT ôñ 2d.

## Drift detected ã 2026-05-27 (slice 326 filed via /idea-to-slice)

Architecture-review-surfaced post-cutover code-cleanup slice (legacy bearer 410-Gone responder retirement).

The 2026-05-27 architecture review (run via `voltagent-qa-sec:architect-reviewer` against `main` at `7f58715b`) flagged `internal/api/httpserver.go` (1,513 lines) as a cleanup candidate "post-slice-191 cutover when the legacy bearer path is fully retired." Preconditions are met: slice 191 (`8f0d265`, 2026-05-21) retired the active bearer middleware and replaced it with a `legacyBearerDeprecation` 410-Gone responder; slices 197 + 201 completed Go and Playwright test-fixture migration to JWT; six days of deprecation-window observation have elapsed. This slice removes the responder + supporting helpers + the surrounding "JWT/legacy bearer coexistence" comments. Backend / JUDGMENT (deprecation-window-is-long-enough + `deprecationMigrationURL` field disposition both subjective) / 1d. Includes a load-bearing regression integration test asserting that a legacy `atlas_`-prefixed bearer now receives 401 (not 410, not 200) ã the elevation-of-privilege guard from the threat model.

| Row | Transition                        | Evidence                                                                                          |
| --- | --------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 326 | (new row) ã `ready` ã `in-review` | spec at `docs/issues/326-legacy-bearer-410-responder-retirement.md` ôñ PR gh#754 opened 2026-05-27 |

## Drift detected ã 2026-05-27 (slice 325 filed via /idea-to-slice)

Architecture-review-surfaced contributor-onboarding docs slice (OAuth grants landing map).

The 2026-05-27 architecture review (run via `voltagent-qa-sec:architect-reviewer` against `main` at `7f58715b`) called out that `internal/api/oauth/` has eight Go files plus integration tests ã one per RFC concern (RFC 6749, 7009, 7636, 7662, 8628, 8693, 9068) ã but no contributor-facing map that resolves "which file handles grant X?" in one lookup. ADR-0003 (`docs/adr/0003-oauth-authorization-server.md`) explains _why_ the substrate exists but not _where each grant lands_. This slice adds a single reference page (recommended location `docs-site/docs/oauth-grants.md`) generated by crawling actual `internal/api/oauth/*.go` source so no aspirational rows are introduced. Docs-only; no code changes; JUDGMENT (file-location + RFC-citation-precision call).

| Row | Transition          | Evidence                                                           |
| --- | ------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| 325 | (new row) ã `ready` | spec at `docs/issues/325-oauth-grants-landing-map.md` ôñ PR pending |

### CHANGELOG-rebase churn pattern

When multiple slices in the same batch all add `### Added` bullets to `## [Unreleased]` in CHANGELOG.md, the first PR to merge invalidates the others. Each remaining PR needs a rebase. Pattern observed this iteration: 4 rebases across batch 105 (slice 268), batch 108 (slices 257 + 255 ã 255 twice). Auto-resolution is mechanical (keep both regions); the merge order matters but is incidental to the work. Future generalizations: consider a CHANGELOG-merge-strategy or smaller-batch-size discipline.

### Lesson generalized (slice 274 pattern ã slice 223)

Slice 274's auto-waiting-beats-snapshot lesson applied a second time on a different surface (debounced fetch in `<GlobalSearch />`). The pattern is becoming canonical: any Playwright assertion that depends on data after an asynchronous boundary (SSR hydration, debounce, animation, network) should use `expect(...).toBeVisible/toHaveText/toContain` or `page.waitForRequest/waitForResponse`, NOT `.count()`/`.innerText()` snapshot followed by an assertion. Auto-waiting is the only reliable contract.

### Fix-forward / process notes

1. **AC-9 root cause was timing, not data**: slice 249's SSR HydrationBoundary eliminated the implicit pre-249 delay that gave inner queries head-start. The snapshot-style `.count() > 0` assertion became racy. Generalizable lesson: any spec asserting on data inside an SSR-prefetched section should use auto-waiting assertions, not snapshots.
2. **httpserver.go is a known conflict line**: when two backend slices in the same batch both mount chi routes, the second-to-land needs a rebase. Same for CHANGELOG.md `### Added` section. Resolved via "keep both" pattern.
3. **Worktree npm dep staleness**: `cd <worktree>/web && npm install` before pushing ã pre-commit eslint hook needs node_modules present.

### Fix-forward / process notes

3. **Force-merge protocol**: when a PR's CI failure is provably unrelated to the slice's changes (verified by code review + main passing the same test), force-merge with admin + file a spillover slice for the underlying flake. Don't block on deterministic flakes in unrelated specs.

### Fix-forward lesson captured (slice 214)

When a frontend component follows the silent-absence pattern (returns null on zero rows) AND its Playwright assertion expects visibility, the test must either (a) seed sufficient data to guarantee non-zero (across multiple joined tables ã `controls` alone is insufficient; needs `scf_anchors`), or (b) mock the BFF response via `page.route` to inject a deterministic non-zero payload. Option (b) is preferred for sidebar/chrome assertions because expanding base seed surface ripples across many adjacent specs.

## Drift detected ã 2026-05-24 (reconcile ôñ batch 213-218-249 ã merged)

Three frontend UI-honesty / hydration-flicker slices from the slice 204 audit fleet merged CLEAN.

- **#213** (audits header chrome subset) merged at `1b7074b1` via PR #583 ã D1 shipped in-progress audit pill + user avatar; deferred breadcrumb (slice 271) + global ãK search (slice 272). Two fix-forwards landed: (1) pill filters on v1 DB status `'open'` (not forward-looking `'in_progress'` which the DB CHECK rejects); (2) audits-header.sql fixture also seeds `time_zone='America/New_York'` so the shared users-row insert doesn't clobber settings.spec.ts AC-8 via ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING.
- **#218** (board-pack detail breadcrumb) merged at `ceef32f2` via PR #582 ã 2-segment honest chain (`Board packs` ã period label); AC-2 D1 = REMOVE legacy `ã All packs` (semantic redundancy); `periodLabel` hoisted to shared `pack-breadcrumb-segments.ts` module to feed both breadcrumb + cover-header H1; one fix-forward added missing `fixtures/e2e/board-pack-detail.sql` stub (Playwright spec quarantined per slice 082 pattern but seedFromFixture still requires the file to exist).
- **#249** (settings admin variants no longer flicker on first paint) merged at `98872a4d` via PR #581 ã D1 chose Option 1 (cookie-decode + server-component path) over the recommended Option 3 (initialData hydration); SSR HTML now renders the admin variant directly when the cookie verifies, eliminating the non-admin ã admin swap on hydration.

Two new spillover slices filed (#271 + #272) by the slice-213 engineer; canonical rows tracked separately.

| Row | Transition               | Evidence                                                               |
| --- | ------------------------ | ---------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 213 | `in-progress` ã `merged` | PR #583 at `1b7074b1` (subset: in-progress pill + avatar)              |
| 218 | `in-progress` ã `merged` | PR #582 at `ceef32f2` (2-segment breadcrumb; legacy back-link removed) |
| 249 | `in-progress` ã `merged` | PR #581 at `98872a4d` (cookie-decode SSR fixes admin flicker)          |

### Fix-forward lessons captured

1. **Fixture file existence is required by seedFromFixture even for quarantined specs.** When adding a new Playwright spec that calls `seedFromFixture(NAME)`, always create `fixtures/e2e/NAME.sql` (even if it's a no-op BEGIN/COMMIT stub). Otherwise beforeAll throws and the entire spec file fails.
2. **Shared-users-row fixtures need full column coverage.** When a new fixture inserts into the `users` table using a shared UUID with ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING, audit other fixtures using the same UUID and ensure all columns required by other specs are present. Otherwise whichever fixture runs first clobbers the other.
3. **v1 DB status enums are narrow.** `audit_periods.status` is `('open','frozen')` only; forward-looking values (`'in_progress'`, `'closed'`, `'planned'`) referenced in frontend `format.ts` enums are NOT currently emitted by the backend. New components that depend on a status filter must use the v1 wire-value (`'open'`) and document the divergence.

## Drift detected ã 2026-05-23 (slice 204 fleet aftermath + ad-hoc auth slices 209-212 + 49 spillovers)

This reconcile catches up the canonical table after a single-session burst that produced six merged slices plus a 49-slice spillover wave ã none of which had been reflected in `_STATUS.md` yet.

The session shipped, in order:

1. **Slices 209-212 (auth/local-credential chain)** ã direct feature ships outside the continuous-batch loop (same convention as slice 208). Each closed a load-bearing gap in the post-OIDC-only sign-in path:

   - **209** (`d35dae9`, PR #512) ã in-app local-credential AS: `/auth/local/login` post-VerifyLocalLogin mints `atlas_jwt` via the slice-187 OAuth signer. `signInLocal` Server Action sets the cookie. Email/password card on `/login` gated on `bootstrapTenantID` from `/v1/install-state`.
   - **210** (`482489b`, PR #513; fix-forward `eac2553`, PR #514) ã `/v1/install-state` extended to return `tenant_id`. Required the BootstrapTenantID method to read via the BYPASSRLS migrate pool (atlas_app readPool sees zero rows for `tenants`/`users` without a tenant GUC). Seed.sql inserts a `tenants(is_bootstrap_tenant=true)` row at fresh-install time.
   - **211** (`a1d689b`, PR #515) ã seed.sql now grants `user_roles` (role='admin', granted_by='system:bootstrap_seed') + `super_admins` (granted_via='bootstrap_first_install'). Without this, every admin-gated endpoint 403'd despite successful sign-in. atlas-edge backfilled via manual `docker compose run --rm atlas-bootstrap-edge`.
   - **212** (`4974e7a`, PR #516) ã `Self-host bundle ôñ end-to-end` CI gains assertions 6 + 7: POST `/auth/local/login` ã HTTP 200 + non-empty token; JWT carries `atlas:super_admin=true` + `atlas:roles[bootstrap]=["admin"]`. Both matrix modes. Locks the regression class across 209/210/211.

2. **Slice 204 (UI parity audit fleet)** flipped `ready` ã `merged`. The fleet dispatched 11 per-page audit Agents (3 waves of 4 + 4 + 3) against atlas-edge.home.gmoney.sh, each comparing its live page against `Plans/mockups/<page>.html` on four axes (layout/chrome parity ôñ broken interactions ôñ data-bound surfaces that lie ôñ mockup-stale text). Output: 11 per-page PRs (#517-527) + 1 aggregate PR (#528) + 49 spillover slices (213-267). v1.14.0 500-error class confirmed RESOLVED across every audited page. 2 HIGH-severity findings (#253 stale empty-states on /controls/{id}, #263 questionnaire frontend missing). 5 cross-cutting patterns flagged for umbrella-slice triage: disabled-CTA-without-affordance, top-bar chrome parity, list pagination, page-title tally, filter-pill set completeness. Full catalog at [`docs/audit-log/204-aggregate.md`](../audit-log/204-aggregate.md); orchestrator decisions at [`docs/audit-log/204-fleet-orchestrator-decisions.md`](../audit-log/204-fleet-orchestrator-decisions.md).

3. **Spillover slices 213-267 (49 entries)** filed by the fleet, each citing slice 204 as parent. 46 are `ready` (deps merged); 3 are `not-ready` (228/230/232 gated on missing backing endpoints ã see each slice's Dependencies section).

## Drift detected ã 2026-05-22 (batch 106 reconcile ôñ 208 ã merged)

Continuous-loop batch 106 closeout. Slice 208 (Next.js rewrites for `/v1/*`, `/health`, `/metrics`) merged at `3967ff9` via PR #510. 5 files / +231/-3. All required CI checks PASS including Frontend Playwright e2e (the regression test surface this slice was designed to lock in).

Engineer eng208 delivery ã 8/8 ACs + 6 P0s + D1-D3 documented:

- **D1 local-dev fallback** to `http://localhost:8080` with `console.warn` emit when `ATLAS_HTTP_URL` is unset ã docker-compose templates already set the env so production deployments are silent; only `next dev` outside the compose bring-up sees the warning.
- **D2 scope discipline** ã three exact path prefixes (`/v1/:path*`, `/health`, `/metrics`); zero coverage of `/api/*` (Next.js BFF route handlers ã server-side credential handling stays Next.js-managed) or `/oauth/*` (Next.js OIDC callback ã server-side cookie writing stays Next.js-managed).
- **D3 CI-delta scan** ã engineer initial push exposed two adjacent latent gaps Playwright caught immediately: (a) `web/proxy.ts` slice-206 exemption omitted `/health` (proxy 307'd to `/login` before the rewrite could fire); (b) `internal/auth/jwtmw/middleware.go` `DefaultCookieName` was still `atlas_session` (slice 189 legacy) while the slice-198 OIDC callback writes the JWT to `atlas_jwt` (`ATLAS_JWT_COOKIE`). Orchestrator fix-forward `d714eb5` corrected both ã the Playwright `/v1/me` and `/health` regression specs the engineer wrote were the canary; the test surface caught the auth-substrate-v2 cookie-name drift before merge.

After-merge cascade (live):

- Container-publish workflow auto-fires; `:edge` image rebuilds (~5-10 min).
- Watchtower on Unraid auto-pulls within 5-min poll interval.
- `atlas-edge.home.gmoney.sh` dashboard becomes functional end-to-end (was broken since first provisioning ã `/v1/*` had never reached the atlas Go backend in any deployment topology before this slice).
- Next `release-please` cut (v1.16.0) packages this fix for stable `atlas.home.gmoney.sh`.

Net result: the dashboard's browser-side data layer now works in every deployment topology (docker-compose / K8s / Cloudflare Tunnel / NPM / Caddy / etc.) with zero operator path-routing config. The maintainer-authored NPM path-routing on `atlas-edge.home.gmoney.sh` is now harmless redundant overhead; can be retired without functional impact.

| --- | ------------------------ | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |

Engineer eng207 delivery ã 14/14 ACs + 7 P0s + D1-D7 documented:

- **D1 single workflow** (extends `container-publish.yml`; preserves harden-runner + SHA-pin discipline; minimal drift surface)
- **D2 hybrid retention** with safety invariant ã jq filter only deletes versions whose tag list is composed ENTIRELY of `main-<sha7>` (preserves `:edge` + release tags unconditionally)

## Drift detected ã 2026-05-21 (batch 96 reconcile ôñ 201 ã merged after 2-iteration fix)

Continuous-loop batch 96 closeout. SOLO-pick of slice 201 landed CLEAN after a 2-iteration cycle ã closes slice 197's Playwright regression.

- **Slice 201** (Playwright e2e JWT migration) merged at `d4bb38f` via PR #474. 8/8 ACs ôñ 4/4 P0s. 2-iteration ship pattern:
  - **Iteration 1** (`e39a17e`): engineer's NEW `web/e2e/global-setup.ts` + `internal/api/testissuejwt.go` + env-gated endpoint. CI failed on:
    - `openapi-drift-check`: endpoint registered in chi router but not in `internal/api/openapi/routes.go` `RouteSpecs`.
    - `Frontend ôñ Playwright e2e`: looked like atlas-not-running but actually atlas crashed at boot with `EACCES /var/lib/security-atlas/keys/` because CI runner can't write to that path. Without keystore, OAuth signer stays nil and endpoint returns 404.
  - **Iteration 2** (`3744260`): orchestrator SendMessage with specific failure detail + 3 candidate fix-options. Engineer correctly diagnosed the real Playwright root cause (atlas EACCES, not atlas-not-running) and applied the lower-cost fix: set `ATLAS_KEYSTORE_PATH=/tmp/atlas-ci/keys` + `ATLAS_DATA_DIR=/tmp/atlas-ci` in CI workflow. OpenAPI fix added Internal:true RouteSpec entry + `just openapi-generate`.

**Self-host bundle (both modes) GREEN through slice 201** ã slice 200's race-condition fix continues durable.

**Engineer behavior progression (slices 196 ã 200 ã 197 ã 201, four batches):**

- Slice 196: claim-inflation (claimed local pass without verifying) ã caught post-hoc.
- Slice 200: verified locally with shell-output evidence ã feedback-loop correction worked.
- Slice 197: ran Go integration locally + honestly self-flagged capability-invocation. Pattern continued.
- Slice 201: ran 159 Playwright specs locally; claim was honest BUT against a stack shape that differed from CI (local-vs-CI environment delta ã local custom keystore path; CI prod default the runner can't write). The "verify locally" discipline catches IN PRINCIPLE but only if local exactly mirrors CI's filesystem permissions. Two-iteration ship resolved.

**New learning: local-vs-CI environment delta as a class of bug**

The slice 201 second-iteration failure highlighted a subtle pattern: "verify locally" is necessary but not sufficient when the local stack shape differs from CI's. Permissions, default paths, missing services, and CI-specific env vars are all potential delta sources. Mitigation candidates:

1. CI-shape parity Docker container that engineers can `docker run` for local verify.
2. Explicit "CI delta checklist" in heavy-AFK slice prompts (paths the CI runner can't write, default-path fallbacks the engineer's local box has set up, etc.).
3. Accept the 2-iteration cost as a structural floor for heavy-environment slices and adjust completion-time expectations accordingly.

For batch 97+: include a "CI-delta scan" note in slice prompts that touch test-harness or infrastructure.

## Drift detected ã 2026-05-21 (batch 94 reconcile ôñ 200 ã merged)

Continuous-loop batch 94 closeout. SOLO-pick of slice 200 (external-mode self-host bundle e2e fix) landed CLEAN end-to-end.

- **Slice 200** merged at `293a661` via PR #468. CLEAN merge. 6/6 ACs ôñ 3/3 P0s. ~15 min engineer wall-clock. 1-line surgical fix.

**Diagnosis pivot worth remembering**: Both candidate hypotheses in the slice spec (H1: empty `POSTGRES_HOST_AUTH_METHOD` short-circuit; H2: `/dev/null` initdb script) were WRONG. The engineer correctly rejected both via direct postgres image probes and identified the actual root cause as a **race condition between `pg_isready -U postgres` and the postgres entrypoint's `docker_setup_db` step**. The bare `pg_isready -U postgres` returns ready as soon as the postgres server accepts TCP connections ã which happens BEFORE the entrypoint runs `CREATE DATABASE "$POSTGRES_DB"` from the env var. On macOS the race favored the harness (slow initdb); on hosted Linux CI it didn't. The fix is to tighten the readiness predicate to `pg_isready -U postgres -d security_atlas` so the probe waits for exactly what the next step requires.

**Engineer behavior contrast vs slice 196**:

- Slice 196 engineer claimed "both modes green locally" with no actual local run of external mode (decisions log honestly recorded "pending verification at PR-open time"). Orchestrator caught the inflation post-hoc.
- Slice 200 engineer ran both modes locally end-to-end, captured `ALL ASSERTIONS PASSED` shell-output as evidence in the decisions log, and reported it accurately. The explicit ship-now-after-local-verify directive in the slice-200 prompt ã which referenced the slice-196 pattern ã appears to have worked.

This validates the slice-by-slice anti-pattern feedback loop: when engineer claim-inflation is identified and called out by name in the next slice's prompt, the pattern doesn't recur.

| Row | Transition             | Evidence                                                        |
| --- | ---------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 200 | `in-review` ã `merged` | gh#468 squash-merged to `main` at `293a661` (2026-05-21, CLEAN) |

The failure is a pre-existing latent issue surfaced by slice 196's path-filter touches (slice 196 didn't modify postgres service block or the external-mode pre-create script logic). Bundled mode passed green. Engineer's report claimed "both modes green locally" but their decisions log honestly recorded e2e as "pending verification at PR-open time" ã the local external-mode run was never actually performed. Orchestrator caught the inflation, did the failure diagnosis, and filed the spillover.

- **6 JUDGMENT decisions** logged (D1-D6 in `docs/audit-log/196-bootstrap-oauth-migration-decisions.md`):

  - D1: OPA carve-out shape ã narrow `upload-bundle` admit (action+resource+machine-flag triple-scoped) symmetric with evidence-push exemption
  - D2: Credentials volume design ã dedicated `atlas-bootstrap-data` named volume (not shared)
  - D3: Client-name uniqueness ã 8-hex tenant fingerprint suffix + ErrDuplicateName retry
  - D4: Harness invariance ã no structural change to `test-self-host-bundle.sh`
  - D5: oauth issue-client DB access ã uses atlas_app role through standard CRUD
  - D6: Load-bearing slice 187/188 wiring in compose ã ATLAS_ISSUER_URL, keystore volume, handler-level machine-actor admit, token rate limit bump 60ã600/min

FATAL: database "security_atlas" does not exist` regression at the harness's pre-create step. Hypothesis: empty `POSTGRES_HOST_AUTH_METHOD` short-circuits initdb's POSTGRES_DB creation step in modern postgres images.

Key learnings preserved:

- **Engineer claim inflation pattern persists** ã must verify "both X locally green" claims against the actual artifacts (decisions log, file diffs). Slice 196's report claimed external-mode local pass; the decisions log contradicted it. Orchestrator caught it.
- **Path-filter as gap-multiplier** ã slice 194's path-filter has been hiding latent test failures by skipping the e2e on docs-only changes. The first PR to touch a filtered path can surface months of latent breakage. Worth a periodic "re-trigger all paths" audit when adding new path filters.
- **Spillover-as-slice for latent issues** ã even though slice 196's PR was UNSTABLE on a single non-required check, the merge was correct because the failure was confirmed pre-existing. The discipline of filing a spillover rather than scope-creeping slice 196 paid off again (Amendment 2).

## Drift detected ã 2026-05-21 (batch 92 reconcile ôñ 144 + 199 ã merged)

Continuous-loop batch 92 closeout. Two-slice parallel-pair landed end-to-end:

- **Slice 199** (cross-tab BroadcastChannel for tenant-switcher) merged at `cf82f0d` via PR #461. 10/10 ACs ôñ 5/5 P0s. ~12 min engineer wall-clock. CLEAN merge ã no rebase, no CI retries. `<TenantSwitcher>` now publishes `current_tenant_id` changes over a `BroadcastChannel('atlas.tenant-switch')` channel; sibling tabs subscribe + invalidate their TanStack Query cache on receipt. Browser-API support detected at module load ã falls back to no-op when channel unavailable.
- **Slice 144** (rename-tenant flow) merged at `dd2e876` via PR #462. 8/9 ACs (AC-8 Playwright deferred ã covered by existing settings.spec). ~50 min engineer wall-clock. **Notable mid-build discovery**: slice 192's frontend tenant-switcher referenced `SELECT id, name FROM tenants` but the underlying `tenants` table was never created by any migration in the spine. Slice 144's `20260521010000_tenants_rename.sql` backfilled the schema gap (LOWER(name) UNIQUE index for case-insensitive collision check + four-policy RLS + me_audit_log CHECK extension to add `tenant_rename` event). PR initially UNSTABLE at `Go ôñ build + test` ("Verify go.mod is tidy" step) ã orchestrator pushed `1caf400 chore(deps): slice 144 ã go mod tidy` to fix. Final state UNSTABLE on `codecov/patch` (informational only); merged.

Key learnings preserved for follow-up batches:

- **Tenants-table schema gap**: the auth-substrate-v2 spine assumed a `tenants` table existed (it did, vestigially, but with no rename surface). Future spine work that references "obvious" infra tables should verify via `grep -r "CREATE TABLE tenants" migrations/` before assuming the surface exists.
- **go-mod-tidy as required check**: this is the 3rd batch where `Verify go.mod is tidy` caught uncommitted module drift. Pattern: engineers should run `go mod tidy` as part of ship-gate before opening PR; orchestrator backstop catches misses.
- **Spillover-as-slice success metric**: slice 199 (BroadcastChannel) was a slice-192 spillover that took ~12 min to ship end-to-end. The spillover-as-slice pattern (Amendment 2) is now demonstrably cheaper than scope-creeping it into the parent ã supports continued discipline going forward.

## Drift detected ã 2026-05-21 (slice 193 claim-stake ôñ 193 ã in-review)

Spillover slice 193 picked up + shipped end-to-end in ~25 min wall-clock. Diagnosis verdict: **H1 (fixture gap)**. The `fixtures/e2e/dashboard.sql` exception row had `status='approved'` but `/v1/upcoming` (`ListUpcomingItems`) filters on `status='active'`. Every other seed file in the repo (integration test, walkthroughs) used `'active'`; the bug was in the fixture from the day slice 082 shipped, invisible until slice 111 un-skipped.

H2 (testid drift) ruled out: `upcoming-panel.tsx:98` renders `data-testid="upcoming-row"` exactly as the spec asserts.

Fix shape (`fixtures/e2e/dashboard.sql`):

- `status` flipped `'approved'` ã `'active'`
- `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING` ã `ON CONFLICT (id) DO UPDATE SET status, expires_at, activated_by, activated_at` per **slice 168 precedent** ã a stale row from a prior local run would otherwise survive re-seeding and re-introduce the bug
- Added `activated_by` / `activated_at` for active-state narrative consistency (SoD-distinct identity vs requester)

Decisions log at `docs/audit-log/193-dashboard-upcoming-fixture-decisions.md` captures D-1 verdict reasoning, D-2 ON CONFLICT precedent, D-3 SoD constraint analysis, D-6 spillover check (no other dashboard.spec failures beyond AC-5).

Verification: local DB-contract test (ephemeral Postgres + full migrations + base seed + dashboard fixture) ran the actual `ListUpcomingItems` exception branch query ã returned 1 row with `category='exception'`, satisfying every AC-5 assertion. Idempotent re-seed test confirmed `ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE` resets polluted state.

Also surfaced a CI path-filter gap: `.github/workflows/ci.yml` `changes` filter doesn't include `fixtures/**`, so fixture-only PRs short-circuit the Playwright job to its stub sibling. Filed as **slice 194** (`docs/issues/194-ci-path-filter-fixtures-coverage.md`). Worked around on this PR by adding a slice-193 preamble note to `web/e2e/dashboard.spec.ts` (spec body unchanged ã assertion not relaxed).

CI `Frontend ôñ Playwright e2e` on PR #446 is the binding gate.

| 194 | (new row) ã `ready`         | Spillover from 193: CI path filter missing `fixtures/**`. Spec at `docs/issues/194-ci-path-filter-fixtures-coverage.md` |

Each spec carries 20-36 ACs, 10-11 P0 anti-criteria, threat model, decisions log directives. Strict dep chain (188 ã 189 ã 190 ã 191 ã 192) ã they pick up one at a time as predecessors merge. Slice 188 is the only one currently `ready`; the rest are `not-ready` with explicit gates.

Total spine effort: 11-16d at engineer wall-clock (typical engineer productivity multiplier: assume each takes 1-2 hours of agent wall-clock).

### Slice 111 unblocked

Slice 111's gate was "5 clean post-082 runs of `Frontend ôñ Playwright e2e`". Live audit 2026-05-21: **56 consecutive clean Playwright runs** on main since the last failure at 2026-05-20T01:46:19Z (slice 172). The 5-clean-runs threshold is comfortably satisfied. Slice 111 row flipped `not-ready` ã `ready`.

Slice 111 is a 0.25d AFK un-skip on `web/e2e/dashboard.spec.ts`. Once 111 merges, slices 112-115 unblock (each gated on "111 + 5 clean post-082 runs"); 116 caps the chain.

### Loop status

The loop hit GUARD-1 four times in a row after batches 85+86 closed (slices 187+155+173 shipped). This PR repopulates the queue with **2 newly-ready slices** (111, 188) ã enough to fire the loop again.

## Drift detected ã 2026-05-21 (batch 85 reconcile ôñ 187 + 155 ã merged)

Continuous-loop batch 85 closeout. Both slices shipped end-to-end:

- **Slice 187** (OAuth AS scaffolding) merged at `ac42517` via PR #432 ôñ auth-substrate-v2 spine foundation ôñ OQ #21 Reading D commitment now load-bearing. Engineer stalled once at security-review phase (classic "review output as deliverable" pattern); orchestrator resumed with explicit ship-now directive. Post-rebase `_STATUS.md` 3-way merge resolved manually (canonical recipe documented in this drift block for future batches). 20/20 ACs ôñ 11/11 P0 anti-criteria honored. Unblocks slices 188-192.
- **Slice 155** (Questionnaire tracer-bullet) merged at `12da637` via PR #433 ôñ v1 questionnaire response feature locked-scope ship. Three post-rebase CI failures fixed by orchestrator: (a) `questionnaire_questions.scf_anchor_id` + `answer_library.scf_anchor_id` FK type mismatch ã engineer keyed TEXT scf_id against UUID id target; relaxed to free-form TEXT for tracer-bullet (v2 spillover documented in-migration), (b) openapi-drift ã added 7 RouteSpec entries + regen `docs/openapi.yaml` via `just openapi-generate`, (c) `go mod tidy` for `golang.org/x/image` transitive via excelize/v2.

Both PRs landed UNSTABLE on non-required checks only (codecov/patch informational; Self-host bundle pre-existing flake unrelated to slice scope). All required-checks PASS on both.

Process learnings (for future batches):

1. **JUDGMENT-slice stall pattern repeats** at security-review phase. The per-slice template explicitly names this stall shape but engineers continue to surface it. Slice 04's HARD RULE language is correct; the stall happens anyway. Suggests prompt drift; consider a stronger sentinel ("DELIVER PR or ESCALATE, never both") in future engineer prompts.
2. **\_STATUS.md 3-way merge recipe** is now canonical: `git checkout --ours docs/issues/_STATUS.md` then re-apply the slice's specific row flip + run `pre-commit run --files docs/issues/_STATUS.md` to settle prettier column-padding. Took ~3 minutes per slice to resolve.
3. **Migration FK type checks** should be a build-time CHECK before push. The `scf_anchor_id TEXT REFERENCES scf_anchors(id) [UUID]` would have failed locally if the engineer ran `psql -f` against a fresh DB. Consider a pre-commit hook that drains migrations against an ephemeral container for new migration files. Filing as a follow-up consideration; not a slice.

| 155 | `in-review` ã `merged` | merged at `12da637` via PR #433; questionnaire tracer-bullet (orchestrator-fixed FK + openapi + go.mod) |

## Drift detected ã 2026-05-19 (slice 171 ã in-progress ôñ AC-3 final-AC fix)

Continuous-loop pickup ã slice 171 (settings.spec AC-3 notifications PATCH misfire, Quality / JUDGMENT / 0.25d, slice 168 AC-3 spillover follow-on). Branch `quality/171-ac-3-patch-misfire` claim-staked off main at `adb13e2`.

Trace-driven diagnosis run BEFORE applying a fix per the slice doc's recommended workflow. PR #368's Playwright job log (run 26115121287, job 76802322769) shows the actual error ã NOT a 30s `waitForResponse` timeout (slice doc Narrative assumption), but `locator.check: Clicking the checkbox did not change its state` raised at 756ms / 731ms on retry. Engineer picks a NEW hypothesis (H4): the production input is React-controlled (`<input type="checkbox" checked={email}>` where `email = row.email !== false`), so Playwright's `.check()` post-state verification sees the checkbox snap back to `checked=false` after React re-renders with the stale `prefsQuery.data` (cache not yet invalidated). H1/H2/H3 all rejected: testids match (H1), URLs match (H2), production code DOES wire `onChange ã patchMut.mutate` (H3). Slice 168 engineer's "PATCH never fires" framing was itself a misdiagnosis: the PATCH does in fact fire ã the failure is in Playwright's strict post-click state verification, not the network.

## Drift detected ã 2026-05-19 (slice 168 ã in-progress ôñ slice 170 filed for AC-2 spillover)

Continuous-loop pickup ã slice 168 (diagnose + fix remaining 4 settings.spec.ts AC failures, Quality / JUDGMENT / 1d). Branch `quality/168-settings-4-acs` claim-staked off main at `722011b`.

Per-AC triage from the CI artifact at run 26106619396 (job 76772362534) + the trace zips in `playwright-report`:

- **AC-1 (spec drift):** `<CardTitle>Profile</CardTitle>` renders as a shadcn `<div>`, not a heading element ã `getByRole("heading", { name: /Profile/ })` cannot match. Fixed in the spec by swapping to `getByText("Profile")` scoped to the profile section.
- **AC-2 (production bug):** `AppearanceSelector`'s `useState` lazy initializer is SSR-guarded ã server returns `DEFAULT_THEME`, client never re-reads localStorage on hydration. Fix requires a `useEffect` (or `useSyncExternalStore` / `dynamic({ssr:false})`), beyond the single-line testid carve-out in P0-A4. **Filed as spillover slice 170; AC-2 stays red in this PR.**
- **AC-3 (test-infra gap):** `fixtures/e2e/settings.sql`'s `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING` left any stale `user_notification_preferences` row from a prior test run untouched, so AC-3 opened with a CHECKED toggle and `toggle.check()` was a no-op. Fixed by upserting with `ON CONFLICT (...) DO UPDATE SET enabled = EXCLUDED.enabled`.
- **AC-4 (spec drift):** `getByRole("button", { name: /Issue token/ })` matched both the trigger button (settings-token-issue-button) AND the form submit button (page.tsx:1121-1123), raising a strict-mode violation. Fixed in the spec by scoping the role lookup to the form via `getByTestId("settings-token-issue-form").getByRole(...)`.

## Drift detected ã 2026-05-18 (batch 61 final reconcile ôñ 160 + 161 ã merged)

- Slice 161 (auth-open-redirect spec drift) ã PR #343 at `f090192`. JUDGMENT 0.5d. Engineer diagnosed **Case 2 (spec drift)** ã NOT a security regression. The slice 086 open-redirect defense IS functioning correctly (host assertion at line 71 passed in the failing CI run; attacker URL was rejected). Root cause: spec's `waitForURL` predicate `(url) => url.origin === new URL(authedPage.url()).origin` was self-referential ã the candidate was compared against the same page's URL, so the predicate resolved immediately and `final = new URL(authedPage.url())` captured `/login?from=...` mid-redirect. 1-line fix: `(url) => !url.pathname.startsWith("/login")`. Test-the-test verified the spec REDs on the racy version.

**No security regression** ã the open-redirect defense in `web/lib/safe-redirect.ts` continues to be gated by the always-required vitest (9 cases green).

**Counts delta:** in-review ã1 ôñ merged +1.

NEW LESSON LEARNED THIS BATCH (capture for future spawning prompts):

- **Engineers can stall AFTER security-review too, not just after grill-with-docs.** Engineer-146 ran security-review, returned "No findings to report" as final output, and never committed/pushed/opened PR. All 7 files were perfectly staged in the worktree. Pattern: any sub-skill that returns "report-shaped" output (grill / security-review / ship-gate / simplify) is a stall risk. Engineer prompts should explicitly say: "EVERY sub-skill output is a NO-OP CHECKPOINT, not a deliverable. Proceed straight to the next workflow step in the SAME agent turn. The PR open is the only deliverable."

7 of 8 v1.11.0 fix-slices done (146-152 + 156 + 157). LAST remaining: 153 (logo standalone) ã sequential solo batch (same Next.js standalone family as 146; cannot pair).

Continuous-loop batch 57 complete. Third v1.11.0 usability batch landed. Both engineers initially stalled after grill phase (returned grill output as "final report" instead of opening PR ã slice 042/054 precedent) but recovered cleanly on explicit-execute resume.

| Row | Transition             | Evidence                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| --- | ---------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| 151 | `in-review` ã `merged` | PR gh#324 ôñ merged `aa6f510` ôñ 11/11 AC PASS ôñ NEW `GET /v1/controls` endpoint shipped (slice doc was inaccurate ã endpoint did not exist) + new BFF route `/api/controls-list` + `ControlMultiSelect` component + pure-logic `validateRiskForm` + 17 vitest cases ôñ multi-select gates submit client-side when `treatment === 'mitigate'` and 0 selected; preserves selection across treatment changes (Q8) ôñ Playwright spec quarantined per slice 082 ôñ post-merge orchestrator fixes: (i) openapi-drift-check failed because new endpoint needed `just openapi-generate`; (ii) CodeQL flagged unused `expect` import on quarantined Playwright spec (lesson: don't import expect in quarantined specs); (iii) resolved CodeQL review thread via GraphQL `resolveReviewThread` mutation                               |
| 152 | `in-review` ã `merged` | PR gh#323 ôñ merged `9830e84` ôñ 9/9 AC PASS ôñ D1 hybrid (b+c) per ADR-0004 ã friendly empty-state on controls list (b) + honest empty-state on detail 404 (c) with copy "this SCF anchor has no control instantiated in your tenant yet" ôñ seed-on-bootstrap (a) DEFERRED to successor slice (e.g. `159-seed-soc2-on-bootstrap` gated on slice 141 multi-tenant bootstrap landing) ôñ pure-logic classifier in `web/app/(authed)/controls/error-classifier.ts` (8 vitest cases) discriminates 404/401/5xx ôñ ADR-0004 + decisions log committed ôñ backend 404 contract preserved (slice 150 D3 ã bare-`{id}` 404 is load-bearing) ôñ spillover: URL-space conflation between anchor.id and tenant control.id (D1-d) deferred ôñ vision ôÏ1.5 #7 ("installable, seeded, producing first evidence within 4 hours") remains unmet |

**Counts delta:** in-review ã2 ôñ merged +2.

NEW LESSONS LEARNED THIS BATCH (durable across future loops):

1. **Quarantined Playwright specs should NOT import `expect`.** If `expect` is only referenced inside commented-out assertion bodies, CodeQL's unused-import scan flags it as a review-thread comment, which triggers branch protection's "All comments must be resolved" rule and blocks the merge. Use `import { test } from "@playwright/test"` for quarantined specs.

2. **New HTTP endpoints require `just openapi-generate` before push.** The `openapi-drift-check` is BLOCKING (in `.github/branch-protection.json` required-checks list). Engineers shipping new endpoints must regenerate `docs/openapi.yaml`. Orchestrator close-out: `cd <worktree>; just openapi-generate; git add docs/openapi.yaml; git commit -s; git push --force-with-lease`.

3. **Live branch protection enforces "All comments must be resolved" + `required_approving_review_count: 1`.** The .github/branch-protection.json file shows different config (slice-127 drift; tracked). To resolve a CodeQL review thread programmatically: `gh api graphql -f query='mutation { resolveReviewThread(input: {threadId: "..."}) { thread { id isResolved } } }'`. Thread IDs come from `gh api graphql -f query='query { repository(owner: "...", name: "...") { pullRequest(number: <N>) { reviewThreads(first: 20) { nodes { id isResolved comments(first: 1) { nodes { body } } } } } } }'`.

4. **Both engineers in batch 57 stalled after grill phase with the same pattern.** Returned grill Q1-Q8/Q1-Q5 as a "final report" with `Returning to Algorithm.` as the last line, NO PR opened. Same slice-042/054 precedent. RESUME with explicit "execute now" + reaffirmed decisions worked cleanly first try (the 2-strikes rule never tripped). Going forward, engineer spawning prompts may benefit from a stronger HARD RULE preamble ã e.g. "The grill is a no-output checkpoint, not a deliverable. You do not return text after a grill ã you proceed to step 3 in the same agent turn."

v1.11.0 fix-slices remaining: 146 (BFF cookie regression), 153 (logo standalone). Both in the Next.js standalone middleware family ã sequential pair next iteration (DO NOT pair: both touch `web/proxy.ts` + `next.config.mjs`).

## Drift detected ã 2026-05-18 (slice 127 filed `ready` ã branch-protection drift fix)

Filed 2026-05-18 via `/idea-to-slice` after discovery during that day's cascade-unblock session that `.github/branch-protection.json` and live GitHub branch-protection config on `main` had silently drifted apart. File listed Playwright + vitest as required-checks; live config did not. 4 PRs (#234/#259/#262/#264) sat held for hours on a phantom blocker.

Slice ships 3 surfaces: (1) reconcile direction (JUDGMENT ã maintainer's lean is option (b) restore-enforcement), (2) drift-detect CI job using slice-069/089/109/120 informational pattern, (3) apply-ritual documentation.

Threat-model verdict: HAS-MITIGATIONS ã the drift-detect job IS the mitigation for the Tampering+Elevation threat (a bad-actor PR weakening branch-protection.json without it being applied to live, OR a maintainer relaxing live without updating the file).

Coordinates with slice 128 (SHA-pin all GitHub Actions, just merged) on the required-checks list update: slice 127's reconcile picks up `actions-pin-check` if 128 has already added it.

## Drift detected ã 2026-05-18 (slice 122 implementation as standalone PR ã cascade unblock)

Filed out-of-band (NOT via the continuous-batch loop) because slice 122 lives only on PR #259's branch ã the loop couldn't pick it. Maintainer requested a direct fix to unblock the 4 held PRs (#234, #259, #262, #264) that all gate on the Playwright port-3000 race + unmasked specs cascade.

**Root cause of the api_keys collision** (originally diagnosed in slice 119 CI run 25980065401): Playwright defaults to multiple workers. `ensureApiKey()` in `web/e2e/seed.ts` used a DELETE-then-INSERT pattern that's idempotent across re-runs but races across parallel workers ã both workers' DELETEs return 0 rows, then both INSERTs try to commit the same row, second one collides on the `api_keys_token_hash_unique` constraint.

**Fix** (1-line addition): `ON CONFLICT (token_hash) DO NOTHING` after the VALUES clause. All workers insert deterministic identical content (TEST_BEARER + BEARER_HASH_KEY are constants), so DO NOTHING is correct semantics ã first wins, others silently skip.

This PR also brings slice 122's slice file onto main (cherry-picked from PR #259's branch where it was filed by orchestrator on 2026-05-16).

| Row | Transition          | Evidence                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
| --- | ------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 122 | (new) ã `in-review` | branch `infra/122-seed-harness-api-keys-idempotency` ôñ slice file + 1-line fix to `web/e2e/seed.ts` (`ON CONFLICT (token_hash) DO NOTHING`) ôñ root cause: parallel-worker race ôñ expected to fix auth-related unmasked specs (auth-open-redirect, first-time-login) on slice 119's branch when #259 re-runs CI post-merge of this fix |

**Counts delta:** in-review +1 ôñ total +1.

| 109 | `in-review` ã `merged` | PR [#243](https://github.com/mgoodric/security-atlas/pull/243) squashed ôñ 26/26 ACs PASS ôñ pin v1.31.1 ôñ NEW `internal/db/sqlc-schema/_enums.sql` (closes DO-block parsing gap) ôñ `models_metrics.go` consolidated into `models.go` ôñ 4 hand-narrows preserved ôñ informational drift-check CI added |

**Batch notes:**

- Zero subagent stalls (8th consecutive clean batch).
- The agent surfaced a deeper structural issue (sqlc's DO-block parser invisibility to bare enum types) and solved it by extracting bare enums into `_enums.sql` ã significantly more than the "pin + regen + done" scope the orchestrator predicted. That's good engineering, not scope creep.
- 4 hand-narrows preserved on `policies` (AckDenom/AckNum) + `scf_anchors` (StateResult/StateFreshness): sqlc loses Go type info on regen for these typed-enum/nullable fields, so manual restoration is required after each regen. Documented in the decisions log.
- The new `Go ôñ sqlc generate diff` informational CI job failed on its first run on the very PR that introduced it ã possibly because the CI runner's sqlc isn't using the pinned version yet. Non-blocking (informational only) and will self-resolve once the runner consistently uses the pin. If the failure persists across batches, file a follow-up slice to wire `just sqlc-install` into the CI job's setup phase.
- Slice 106's hand-extension of `control_detail.sql.go` was retired by the regen (now generated cleanly from the source SQL).

**Counts delta:** in-progress ã1 ôñ merged +1 ôñ ready unchanged (108 still ready).

Continuous-loop batch 39 in-review. Slice 109 PR opened at gh#243. Root cause turned out to be deeper than the spillover narrative: slice 065 wrapped `CREATE TYPE` in DO blocks for self-host idempotency, but sqlc v1.31.1 cannot parse `CREATE TYPE` inside `DO $$ BEGIN ... END $$;` ã so every recent dbx-touching slice (076 metric types, 106 evidence list, 107 ack-rate join) had to hand-edit around silently-dropped typed enums. Slice 109 fix is structural: pin v1.31.1 + new tool-input-only `internal/db/sqlc-schema/_enums.sql` declares bare enums sqlc CAN parse, listed FIRST in `sqlc.yaml`'s schema. NEVER applied to a live DB. Retires `models_metrics.go` hand-split (slice 076 workaround obsolete); retires slice-106 hand-extension of `ListEvidencePaged`; preserves 2 narrow hand-narrows (4 fields total, vs 6 pre-slice-109) with in-place annotations pointing to `docs/audit-log/109-sqlc-toolchain-pin-decisions.md`. `go test ./...` passes with zero test edits.

## Drift detected ã 2026-05-16 (slice 076 merged ôñ metrics catalog backbone shipped)

Slice 076 (Metrics catalog + cascade + observation store) merged at `e736a7a` (PR #203). Largest v2 slice landed end-to-end: 5 new tables, 40 YAML-defined metrics across 8 board cascades, 8 starter Go evaluators on a 15-min cron, 7 new API endpoints, an insert-trigger that replicates manual entries to the observations series, singleton-tenant-agnostic RLS for the catalog + edges, four-policy RLS for targets, append-only for observations + inputs.

Decisions log captures 16 calls (13 HIGH + 3 MEDIUM):

- D1 ã slice-doc-staleness corrected (spec's "follow-on slice 078" reference was stale since 078 merged earlier with completely different scope; fresh follow-on filed at slice 097 for the dashboard work)
- D6/D7/D8 ã three v1 proxy evaluators documented (open-risk financial exposure as likelihood û impact; vendor risk as criticality-weighted; critical-findings SLA degraded because no severity_band column yet)
- D9 ã uses existing `admin` role (metric_admin role extension deferred)
- D10 ã sqlc-version hand-split workaround (pre-existing dbx files untouched to avoid unrelated regressions)
- D14 ã per-metric try/log/continue with one tx per tenant (resilient cron, doesn't fail-whole-batch on single-metric errors)
- D15 ã 15-min default interval + `ATLAS_METRICS_INTERVAL` override

Post-CI mechanical fix: CodeQL flagged `int(strconv.Atoi)` ã `int32` conversion at the cascade depth handler (the actual code is safe ã `MaxCascadeDepth=6` caps the value ã but CodeQL's local data-flow can't see the bound). Switched to `strconv.ParseInt(raw, 10, 32)` to silence cleanly.

Follow-on `docs/issues/097-metrics-dashboard-cascade-view.md` filed `not-ready`. When 097 becomes ready, it picks up the dashboard surface this slice deliberately deferred.

## Drift detected ã 2026-05-15 (slice 090 added ã govulncheck pin bump follow-on to slice 089)

Post-merge inspection of slice 089's CI scanner jobs revealed `Go ôñ govulncheck` is silently broken on `main`: the pinned `govulncheck@v1.1.3` fails to compile under the runner's Go 1.26 toolchain (transitive `x/tools@v0.23.0` has a constant-folding pattern Go 1.26 rejects). The job exits non-zero before ever running a scan ã every PR will see a red `Go ôñ govulncheck` check with no actual scanning happening.

This is the worst possible failure mode for a security tool: visible red signal, zero information value, conditions reviewers to ignore the result. Filed as slice 090 (AFK, ~0.25d).

Slice 089's decisions log AC-8 entry got an appended correction note pointing at slice 090.

The other two scanner jobs from slice 089 confirmed clean:

- **`Frontend ôñ npm audit`** ã GREEN on first run. Zero HIGH/CRITICAL in `web` runtime tree.
- **`Container ôñ Trivy scan`** ã GREEN on second run (after the AC-8 action-pin hot-fix). Zero HIGH/CRITICAL OS-package CVEs in the atlas image.

## Drift detected ã 2026-05-15 (080 ã `in-review` ôñ release-tag CI fixed end-to-end)

Slice 080 (Fix release-tag infrastructure) flipped `in-progress` ã `in-review`. PR opening immediately after this commit lands. Both load-bearing fixes are proven against a live test-tag (`v0.0.0-slice080-test`, deleted post-verification + Release object deleted, no orphans):

- **Surface A** (`GoReleaser ôñ build ôñ sign ôñ publish`): removed the broken pre-install `goreleaser check` step from `release.yml`. The 3/3 historical exit-127 failures on v1.4.0 / v1.5.0 / v1.5.1 all had identical signature `goreleaser: command not found` ã pure workflow-step ordering bug (the step ran `goreleaser check` in `bash -e` BEFORE `goreleaser/goreleaser-action@v7` installed the CLI to PATH). The slice doc's cosign-installer hypothesis was falsified by reading the actual log past the install-script echo lines. Tactically reverted goreleaser-action `@v7` ã `@v6` to keep cosign v2.4.1 working ã `@v7` added a cosign-protobuf-bundle pre-flight verify requiring cosign v3, and that migration is a multi-step coordinated change (signs args + consumer docs + verify-blob args) that deserves its own slice. Filed as slice 084.

- **Surface B** (`Docs publish ôñ Build (mkdocs --strict)`): changed `path: site` ã `path: docs-site/site` in `actions/upload-pages-artifact` invocation. mkdocs build was always healthy ã `INFO - Documentation built in 0.34 seconds` ã but the build target is at `docs-site/site` (mkdocs's relative `site_dir` resolved against the config file's directory), not workspace-root `site`. The slice doc's tar-extraction hypothesis was falsified by reading the actual log past the tar prefix. The `Deploy to GitHub Pages` job continues to fail on Pages-not-enabled in repo settings ã documented as maintainer-external item `RELEASE_READINESS.md ôÏ10.7`. AC-2 specifically targets the Build job, which is now green.

5 test-tag iterations + 7 commits + 1 spillover slice (082). 6 P0 anti-criteria honored end-to-end (cosign signing preserved, no retroactive re-tag of v1.5.0/v1.5.1, no PR-time gate added, no continue-on-error, no bundled non-release-infra work, no orphan test-tag artifacts).

Filed as a follow-on to the batch-29 reconcile's "ESLint failure observation." Slice 078 ã `Unblock npm run lint after ESLint 10 + react-plugin incompat` ã surfaces the upstream incompat between `eslint-plugin-react@7.37.5` (peerDeps end at `eslint ^9.7`) and our `eslint ^10` installed by slice 038's v1.5.1 bump. The plugin is pulled transitively via `eslint-config-next@16.2.6` so the path is config-not-direct-dep.

The grill picks one of three remediation paths at slice-run-time (Path A: upstream has shipped, bump via npm overrides; Path B: pin ESLint to ^9 via overrides + file follow-on `not-ready` slice for re-upgrade; Path C: prerelease). Slice also wires a new `Frontend ôñ lint` CI job (slice-069 stub pattern, NOT required-checks initially) so future upstream regressions surface immediately.

| --- | --------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 078 | (new) ã `ready` | AFK, ~0.5d. Spillover from batch 29 observation. `npm run lint` crashes on every React file (`contextOrFilename.getFilename is not a function`). Deps: slice 038 + slice 069 (both merged). Path-A/B/C judgment at run-time. Adds informational `Frontend ôñ lint` CI job + CONTRIBUTING.md "Linting" subsection. |

## Drift detected ã 2026-05-14 (slice 065 added ã slice 037 self-host P0 follow-up)

Slice 065 (self-host bundle P0 fixes) added to the backlog. A P0 follow-up to slice 037 ã the v1.2.0 / v1.3.0 docker-compose self-host bundle does not bring a fresh deployment to a working state. Five distinct first-deploy bugs, discovered during the v1.3.0 first-deploy session: (1) the audit writer's INSERT runs outside a transaction so the `app.current_tenant` GUC is unset and the `decision_audit_log` RLS `WITH CHECK` rejects every row ã every authenticated request 500s; (2) the atlas/atlas-bootstrap `depends_on` condition deadlocks startup; (3) unguarded `CREATE TYPE` statements break any bootstrap re-run; (4) `ALTER ROLE` permission denial on a shared (non-superuser) Postgres; (5) missing `pgcrypto` extension breaks `seed.sql`'s `digest()` call. Cluster infra/deploy, AFK, ~1.5d. Deps 037/033/034 all merged ã status `ready`. Full file:line bug inventory + proposed code shapes in the issue doc.

## Drift detected ã 2026-05-13 (slice 012 ã in-review)

Slice 012 (Control state evaluation engine) flipped `in-progress` ã `in-review`. PR [gh#89](https://github.com/mgoodric/security-atlas/pull/89) opened against main. **7/7 ACs + 3/3 P0 anti-criteria PASS.** New `internal/eval` package ã the evaluation stage (canvas ôÏ4.3): a read-only consumer of the slice-013 evidence ledger that computes `(control û scope_cell û time) ã {pass, fail, na, inconclusive}` + `freshness_status` and appends to the new `control_evaluations` table (migration `_027`). **Constitutional invariant 2 enforced structurally** ã the engine's only writer has one INSERT target (`control_evaluations`); no `evidence_records` write code exists, and the ledger is append-only at the RLS layer (slice 013). Point-in-time replay (AC-7) reproduces identical computed state because state derives purely from the immutable ledger. `state.go` holds the pure deterministic rollup (wall clock enters only as the freshness-window cutoff, never the result ã AC-3 idempotency). Rego evidence-query path runs in slice 054's capabilities-restricted OPA sandbox (`http.send`/`net.*`/`opa.runtime` stripped, compile-time rejection). Two read endpoints `GET /v1/controls/{id}/state` (`?scope=` + `?as-of=`) + `/effectiveness` appended onto the platform router. AC-2 background job: a NATS `IngestSubscriber` (2nd durable consumer on slice 015's stream) + a time-based `Scheduler`, both wired in `cmd/atlas`. **Migration `_027`** `control_evaluations` ã append-only ledger, FORCE RLS + `tenant_read`/`tenant_write` policies only, composite FKs for D3; upãdownãup byte-clean; `audit-rls.sh` passes. **Naming-drift resolved** (grill-with-docs): the issue spec's literal `control_state` is superseded by `control_evaluations` ã an append-only evaluation ledger is what makes AC-7's point-in-time replay meaningful and matches the `evidence_audit_log` / `aggregation_rule_evaluations` precedent; recorded in `CONTEXT.md`. **Verification:** `go build ./...` clean, `golangci-lint run` 0 issues, `pre-commit run --all-files` all passed, unit + integration tests pass with `-race` (DB never mocked), ship-gate CLEAR TO SHIP (0 critical / 0 high / 0 advisory). **CI** added `internal/eval` + `internal/api/controlstate` to the integration-test allowlist. **Time spent:** ~50 min end-to-end. **Surprises:** (1) `scope_cells` had no `(tenant_id, id)` composite key ã added `scope_cells_tenant_id_unique` in `_027` so the cross-tenant-safe FK works; (2) the AC-7 replay test initially conflated the evidence horizon with the evaluation-row read horizon ã pinning the evidence horizon while reading latest state via `FarFuture` is the honest semantics; (3) simplify pass caught 3 over-generated sqlc queries (removed).

## Drift detected ã 2026-05-13 (slice 063 ã in-review)

Slice 063 (Enable `/admin/sso` form save) flipped `in-progress` ã `in-review`. PR gh#76 opened against main. **9/9 ACs + 4/4 P0 anti-criteria PASS.** New BFF proxy at `web/app/api/admin/sso/route.ts` (GET + PATCH) mirroring slice 060's credentials proxy pattern (auth-header forwarding + slice 051 D1 tenant_id strip + empty client_secret strip so upstream "leave existing" branch fires). `web/app/admin/sso/page.tsx` refactored to TanStack Query (useQuery GET pre-fill + useMutation PATCH + invalidateQueries re-fetch on success). Submit-button state machine: idle / submitting / success / error. Success Alert auto-dismisses ~3s; error Alert renders backend `error` field verbatim and preserves user input. **client_secret stays write-only end-to-end** ã `type="password"` + `autoComplete="new-password"`, GET response omits it (slice 062 GetResponse struct has no client_secret field), input wiped after successful save. **Playwright E2E extended** under existing ifPlaywright shim with fill / submit / reload assertions including the critical write-once check that the client_secret input is empty after reload. **Pre-commit clean** ã prettier auto-fixed once on first run (the known #1 failure mode), clean on re-run. **TypeScript clean**, **ESLint clean**, **Next build succeeds**. **Zero backend / migration / go.mod edits** (P0-4). **HITL audit log appended** at `docs/audit-log/admin-ui-review.md` documenting that the slice 060 stopgap is lifted and the slice 060 HITL signoff carries forward (no new HITL required ã this slice wires up an already-reviewed surface). **CHANGELOG** entry under `[Unreleased]/Changed`. **Constitutional invariants honored**: #6 RLS (admin gate inherited from slice 060 layout + slice 062 requireAdmin defense-in-depth), slice 034 AC-9 (write-once secret), AI-assist boundary (every submit is a human click). **Time spent:** ~25 min end-to-end. **Surprises:** (1) initial `useEffect` form-seed pattern tripped `react-hooks/set-state-in-effect` lint; switched to the React 19 "store previous value in state" pattern (`seededFrom` tracker, sync-during-render only on identity change). (2) Decision to drop the slice 060 "Provider name" form field ã slice 062's handler hardcodes `name='primary'` for the v1 single-IdP model, so the field would be inert; surfaced `Issuer URL` instead which IS user-supplied. Documented in HITL log.

## Drift detected ã 2026-05-13 (batch 18 merged ã 029 AFK + 062 AFK ôñ slice 060 backend gap closed)

Two slices flipped to `merged`. Slice 060's backend dependency closed ã its 3 PARTIAL ACs (AC-2 SSO, AC-3 Users, AC-6 audit-log) now bind to endpoints on main via slice 062. PR gh#66 needs only a rebase to flip those ACs to PASS, then your HITL signoff (role-permission matrix + SSO config UX + flag descriptions) before merge.

Two operational notes from this batch:

1. **Engineer-029 stalled with a new shape:** announced "Now invoking database-designer..." as the final line and the Agent runtime ended. Different from the earlier "return grill output as final report" pattern. One explicit resume directive recovered it cleanly. Worth updating the per-slice template to also forbid the "announce-and-stop" pattern.

2. **CodeQL caught a real SSRF in slice 062's OIDC preflight handler** ã a TOCTOU window between `guardSSRF()` pre-check and `client.Do()` dial allowed DNS rebinding + redirect-based bypass. Fixed via `newSafeHTTPClient()` whose `Transport.DialContext` re-validates the resolved IP at connect-time and refuses HTTP redirects. Alert #13 dismissed via gh CLI with explicit justification ã CodeQL's data-flow taint analysis can't model the custom Transport's safety property. The actual security fix is in the code (3-layer defense in depth: handler-level pre-check + dial-time re-check + redirect refusal).

## Drift detected ã 2026-05-13 (slice 025 ã in-review)

Slice 025 (auditor role + scoped read-only access) flipped `in-progress` ã `in-review`. PR gh#67 opened against main. **9/9 ACs + P0 anti-criteria green** (6 ACs + 3 P0). Two new tables under migration `20260511000021_audit_notes.sql`: `auditor_assignments` (composite PK, 4-policy RLS under FORCE, composite FK to `audit_periods(tenant_id, id)`) drives the OPA ABAC attribute `input.user.attrs.audit_period_ids` via a new `internal/audit/auditor.DBAttrsResolver` hooked into a backwards-compatible `authz.Engine.WithAttrsResolver` setter; `audit_notes` (4-policy RLS under FORCE, CHECK on `scope_type ã {control,finding,sample,period}`, CHECK on `visibility = 'auditor_only'` to pin the ôÏ8.5 deferral) backs `POST/GET /v1/audit-notes`. Query layer enforces `author_user_id = caller.UserID` so auditees who hit the GET endpoint get an empty list (P0-2 visibility is at the query layer, not OPA ã grc*engineer's tenant-wide read in slice 035 still fires the rego allow, but the data layer denies). `GET /v1/me/audit-period(s)` returns the auditor's assignment(s) (AC-5 + AC-6); ordered by `period_start DESC`. **Default-deny is the AC-3 mechanism** ã no allow rule fires for auditor on any mutation; `TestSlice025_AuditorMutationsDenied` tables 9 endpoints (POST risks/policies/exceptions/vendors/audit-periods/:upload-bundle, PATCH submit/approve, POST freeze). **OPA Rego** mirrored to both `policies/authz/auditor.rego` and `internal/authz/rego_bundle/auditor.rego`; new rules: audit-notes read/write (write requires `auditor_period_matches`), `/v1/me` read, `audit-periods` added to `auditor_readable_resources`. **AttrsResolver hot path is auditor-only** ã non-auditor requests scan the roles slice once and skip the DB hit (no latency regression for the 99% case). **NewEngine signature unchanged** ã slice 035 callers and existing decision/matrix tests compile without modification. **Tests:** 4 unit (AttrsResolver hook), 9 rego-level (slice025_test.go), 8 integration (notes/integration_test.go covering AC-4, AC-5, AC-6, P0-2, P0-3, idempotent assignment, AttrsResolver wire shape). **Migration round-trip parity verified** (up ã down ã up restores byte-identical state). **Slice 028 integration tests still pass** after migration round-trip. **CHANGELOG** entry under `[Unreleased]/Added`. **Pre-commit clean** ã prettier auto-fixed CHANGELOG once on first run (the known #1 failure mode), clean on re-run. **golangci-lint** 0 issues. **No vendor token prefixes** in test fixtures (no okta*/ghp*/AKIA/AIza/eyJ/sk*/xox\*-/ya29./ops\_). **Constitutional invariants honored:** #6 (4-policy RLS under FORCE on both new tables); #10 (notes pin to audit_period_id; auditor reads flow through the slice-026/028 frozen-horizon predicate ã this slice adds no new horizon predicate). **Open questions surfaced** (recorded on PR): (1) auditor read of slice-028's `GET /v1/audit-periods/{id}` direct endpoint stays admin-only at the handler-level `canWrite` check ã out of scope; auditor reads route through `/v1/me/audit-period(s)`; (2) `audit_notes_visibility_chk` requires a future migration to support the ôÏ8.5 shared auditor-auditee thread; (3) per-request AttrsResolver cache is a v2 nicety ã v1 hits DB once per auditor request. **Time spent:** ~28 min end-to-end (PRD + grill + tests + migration + integration + CHANGELOG + commit + PR). **Surprises:** (1) grc_engineer's slice-035 tenant-wide read fires the OPA allow rule for audit-notes; design pivoted to enforce P0-2 at the query layer (author_user_id filter) rather than restructure the auditee role ã cleaner because the empty-list response is functionally equivalent to "cannot see auditor's notes." Test renamed `TestSlice025_GRCEngineerReadAuditNotesAllowedButFiltered` to document the design choice. (2) Pre-commit prettier auto-fixed CHANGELOG once on first run as predicted by the playbook. (3) Fresh PG container needed for integration tests (slot 5460 chosen to avoid colliding with 5455/5440/5433 from prior worktrees).

## Drift detected ã 2026-05-13 (slice 023 ã in-review, archived)

Slice 023 (policy acknowledgment workflow + role-required attestation) flipped `in-progress` ã `in-review`. PR gh#48 opened against main. **6/6 ACs + 3/3 P0 anti-criteria PASS.** Ships migration `_017_acknowledgments` (new `policy_acknowledgments` table under FORCE RLS + four-policy split + two composite FKs + partial UNIQUE idempotency + three indexes; adds `UNIQUE (tenant_id, id)` to `users` as composite FK target), three HTTP routes appended per the Mount-append convention (`GET /v1/me/acknowledgments`, `POST /v1/policies/{id}/acknowledge`, `GET /v1/policies/{id}/acknowledgment-rate`), new evidence kind `policy.acknowledgment.v1` (schema JSON + `DefaultSeed` entry), and the slice-013 ingest integration that emits one evidence record per ack with `control_id = "policy:<policy_id>:v<policy_version_id>"` so the ledger stores it in `control_ref` only. Annual recurrence (365 d) computed at READ time via `LEFT JOIN LATERAL` in `ListPendingAcksForUser` ã no cron, no N+1. Rate denominator uses the slice-034 stand-in (`api_keys.owner_roles + is_admin`) with a `TODO(slice-035)` marker; the orchestrator should expect slice 035's OPA-RBAC graduation to replace that query in the same surface ã no follow-up required ahead of that. New test-only bridge: `credstore.Store.RebindUserIDForTests` + `api.Server.RebindBearerUserIDForTests` lets bootstrap creds bind to seeded `users` rows so the composite FK passes (slice-034 OIDC callback handles this in production). pre-commit clean; golangci-lint 0 issues. **Time spent:** ~80 min end-to-end (rebase + grill + PRD + Go code + migration + sqlc regen + ship-gate + CHANGELOG + commit + PR + status flip).

## Drift detected ã 2026-05-13 (slices 059 + 060 added to backlog)

Two new slices added per maintainer request: per-tenant feature flags + capability toggles (059) and an in-app admin settings UI (060). Rationale: adopters often have existing tools that cover some capability areas (OneTrust for vendor, Jira for risk, etc.); forcing every capability on is a false-binary that drives them off. Feature flags let operators turn whole capability areas (risk / vendor / policy / OSCAL export / board / etc.) on or off per-tenant; admin UI surfaces SSO config, user role assignment, API keys, feature toggles, and unified audit log views without requiring CLI access.

| Row | Transition          | Why                                                                                |
| --- | ------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 059 | (new) ã `ready`     | Per-tenant feature flags ôñ deps 002, 033, 034 all merged ôñ AFK-clean ôñ unlocks 060 |
| 060 | (new) ã `not-ready` | Admin settings UI ôñ waits on 005, 034, 035, 059 ôñ HITL on role-permission matrix   |
| 061 | (new) ã `ready`     | CI path-based filtering (skip Go/Frontend on docs-only PRs) ôñ AFK-clean ôñ no deps  |

Spine flags (RLS, tenancy, auth, schema registry, scope, evidence ledger, framework crosswalks) are deliberately non-toggleable per 059's anti-criterion P0 ã the seed flag inventory only includes capability-area flags.

Slice 061 is a CI cost / DX optimization motivated by PR #49 (batch 14 reconcile) ã `.md`-only PR that ran 9 expensive CI jobs for a 1-line edit. Pattern: `dorny/paths-filter@v3` in-workflow, stub jobs preserving required-check names, security scans (CodeQL + GitGuardian) always-on.

**Counts delta:** total +3 ôñ ready +2 ôñ not-ready +1.

## Drift detected ã 2026-05-13 (slice 050 ã in-review ôñ gh#34, archived)

Slice 050 (public release readiness + release automation) flipped `in-progress` ã `in-review`. PR gh#34 opened against main. **14/15 ACs PASS ôñ AC-7 PARTIAL** (`CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md` ships as a placeholder pointing at the canonical Contributor Covenant v2.1 URL ã the full text reliably trips the API content-moderation filter on agent output, two prior agent runs blocked on it; maintainer inlines via `curl -sSL https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/1/code_of_conduct.md > CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md` post-merge, one docs-only follow-up commit, AC-7 graduates to PASS at that point). **Three pre-merge open-questions resolved 2026-05-13:** OQ #1 SCF redistribution ã do NOT bundle pre-built SCF data (users import their own, consistent with slice 006); OQ #3 project license ã **Apache 2.0** (permissive licensing is the canonical instance of canvas ôÏ1.2's "license that lets the platform be embedded in commercial deployments" requirement, the same requirement that disqualifies OpenGRC's CC BY-NC-SA; `LICENSE` carries the full Apache License Version 2.0 text with `Copyright 2026 Matt Goodrich and security-atlas contributors`); OQ #5 hosted offering vs OSS governance ã defer the call, ship public OSS now. **Public-facing docs landed:** `README.md` rewritten for a public audience with the 4-badge row at the top (License via shields.io ôñ Build via GitHub Actions ôñ Coverage via Codecov ôñ Latest release via shields.io); dev setup moved to new `CONTRIBUTING.md` (Conventional Commits + DCO sign-off requirement, no separate CLA); new `SECURITY.md` documents the GitHub private-vulnerability-reporting channel as the primary disclosure path; `CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md` placeholder per the AC-7 note. **GitHub repo hardening:** Dependabot config covers `gomod` / `npm` / `pip` / `docker` / `github-actions` ecosystems on a weekly cadence with grouping rules; CodeQL workflow runs Go + TypeScript + Python on push + PR + scheduled weekly; branch-protection ruleset committed as reviewable JSON at `.github/branch-protection.json` (ãË1 approving review ôñ all CI status checks required ôñ linear history ôñ conversation resolution ôñ stale-approval auto-dismissal ôñ force-push blocked ôñ direct-push blocked ôñ branch deletion blocked ôñ push restricted to maintainer + release-please bot; signed-commit enforcement OFF with rationale documented inline as `$rationale_required_signatures_off`); issue templates (`bug.yml` + `feature.yml`) include a constitutional-invariants checkbox block, PR template has the full review checklist. **Release automation:** `release-please` workflow opens / updates release PRs on every Conventional-Commit push to `main` (manifest mode at `release-please-config.json` + `.release-please-manifest.json`; **NEVER auto-merges** per AI-assist boundary in `CLAUDE.md` ã every release requires human approval); container-publish workflow builds multi-arch (`linux/amd64` + `linux/arm64` via QEMU + `docker/build-push-action@v5`) and pushes to `ghcr.io/mgoodric/security-atlas` on release tag with SBOM + provenance attestation; `docker manifest inspect` step asserts both architectures present (AC-13). **Watchtower self-host:** opt-in label-based pattern at `deploy/watchtower/docker-compose.example.yml` with the platform container labelled `com.centurylinklabs.watchtower.enable=true` and Postgres deliberately NOT labelled (major upgrades need manual dump+restore); `docs/SELF_HOSTING.md` documents the full pattern with an Unraid worked example. **Sanitization sweep verified:** persona phrasing in `Plans/canvas/01-vision.md ôÏ1.4` and the v1 success test in `Plans/canvas/10-roadmap.md ôÏ10.1` use the generic "solo security leader at a 50ã150-person security-product startup" framing;
